old_claim.note: Hospital Information Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains from magnetic_resonance_imaging to hospital_information_systems; clinical information systems are deployment infrastructure rather than part of the MRI physical method.

source_shape.source_locator: Nobel 2003 press-release description of MRI as NMR-based imaging using magnetic fields, radio waves, and reconstruction.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes the method basis for MRI through nuclear magnetic resonance and image reconstruction, not through hospital information-system infrastructure.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This rejects a direct HIS prerequisite because the source's method account explains MRI without any hospital information-system component, while HIS belongs to clinical workflow around imaging.

ontology_before: MRI included hospital information systems as a direct prerequisite, mixing a later clinical workflow system with the imaging modality.
ontology_after: MRI is scoped to NMR-based image acquisition and reconstruction; hospital information systems remain separate clinical infrastructure.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: hospital information systems no longer appear as a direct MRI prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The MRI node were re-scoped to a hospital PACS/HIS deployment workflow rather than the imaging modality.
A source showed MRI acquisition physically requires a hospital information system.
Removing the edge also removed all clinical context from MRI instead of only the direct HIS prerequisite.
short_reason: Hospital information systems support clinical operations but are not a prerequisite for MRI technology.
